steps.title: Identify the sentence structure
steps.content: The sentence begins with subject “Trisyllabic words” and ends with what should be the main verb “consist.” The blank must contain either a finite verb (A, B, C) or a participle to complete the thought.

steps.title: A participle reduces a relative clause without creating multiple main verbs
steps.content:
“Classified” is a past participle that modifies “Trisyllabic words,” functioning as a reduced relative clause (“which are classified”) and leaving “consist” as the sole main verb.

The other three options use finite verbs that create a second independent clause or verb phrase fragment without a clear subject, leaving the sentence structure ambiguous.

That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
